debris_field.name: The Debris Field
debris_field.description: A slowly tumbling mass of broken hull plating, cargo containers, and twisted metal. The remains of some great vessel, shattered and scattered across this pocket of void.
debris_field.aspect: A Dead Ship Still Full of Secrets

rooms.name: Edge of the Debris Field
rooms.description: Jagged metal fragments drift lazily at the boundary of the debris field. A faint hum resonates through the larger pieces, as if the wreckage still remembers being whole. The way back to the skerry is clear to the south.
aspects: Everything Drifts If You Let Go

rooms.name: Shattered Cargo Bay
rooms.description: The remnants of a cargo hold, its walls peeled open like a tin can. Crates bob gently in the void, some still sealed. A young woman waves from atop one of the larger crates.

rooms.name: Gaping Hull Breach
rooms.description: A massive hole torn through the outer hull reveals layers of internal structure. The edges are sharp and corroded. A corroded automaton jerks erratically among the exposed girders, sparks flying from its joints.

rooms.name: Ruined Control Room
rooms.description: Banks of dead consoles line the walls, their screens dark and cracked. Someone has cleared a workspace near the captain's chair, tools laid out in precise rows.
aspects: Just Enough Power to Be Dangerous

rooms.name: Engine Room Wreckage
rooms.description: The heart of the ship, now still. Massive turbines lie split open, their cores dark. Among the wreckage, a faintly glowing device pulses with residual energy — the Stabilization Engine.
aspects: The Heart Stopped But It's Still Warm

enemies_data.name: Rat Swarm
enemies_data.description: A chittering mass of pale, eyeless rats with translucent skin stretched tight over visible bones. They pour from cracks in the bulkhead like liquid, each one small but the whole swarm a gnawing tide.

enemies_data.name: Hound of Annwn
enemies_data.description: A spectral hound with white fur and red-tipped ears, its form flickering between solid and translucent. It watches you with ancient, knowing eyes, and its growl resonates in your chest.
aspects: Between This World and the Next

enemies_data.name: Corroded Automaton
enemies_data.description: A maintenance robot gone wrong. Its corroded frame jerks with erratic, dangerous movements. Sparks fly from exposed wiring.
aspects: Doesn't Know What It's Doing Anymore
One Spark From a Short Circuit

coral_thicket.name: The Coral Thicket
coral_thicket.description: A dense growth of crystallized organic matter, glowing with soft bioluminescence. The coral structures twist and branch in impossible patterns, creating a labyrinth of beauty and danger.
coral_thicket.aspect: Everything Here Grows Toward Something Hungry

rooms.name: Coral Threshold
rooms.description: The edge of the thicket glows with pale blue light. Crystalline tendrils reach outward like fingers, and the air tastes faintly sweet. The path back to the skerry lies to the west.
aspects: Beautiful Enough to Drop Your Guard
The Sweetness Wants Something From You

rooms.name: Luminous Grove
rooms.description: A clearing in the coral where enormous fan-shaped growths create a canopy of shifting light. Someone has been living here — there's a crude lean-to built against a coral wall.
aspects: Someone Lived Here For a Reason

rooms.name: Narrow Coral Tunnel
rooms.description: A tight passage between coral walls, barely wide enough to squeeze through. The walls pulse with internal light, and thorny protrusions scrape against anything that passes.
aspects: No Room to Swing a Sword

rooms.name: Spore Chamber
rooms.description: A vast hollow space filled with drifting clouds of luminous spores. They pulse in slow rhythms, beautiful but potentially toxic. The air is thick and warm.
aspects: Breathe Wrong and You'll See Things
The Warmth Makes You Want to Stay

rooms.name: Heart of the Thicket
rooms.description: The deepest part of the coral growth, where the crystal structures are oldest and most complex. A woman tends a small garden of cultivated coral, humming to herself. Nestled among the roots, a lattice-work of living crystal pulses with green light.

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler
enemies_data.description: A coral-armored predator that blends with the thicket walls. It strikes from ambush, its barbed limbs lashing out with surprising speed.
aspects: Strikes Before You Know It's There
Part of the Wall Until It Isn't

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler Alpha
enemies_data.description: The largest of its kind, this crawler has grown thick coral plating and developed a cunning patience. It waits until prey is committed before striking.
aspects: It Can Wait Longer Than You Can

enemies_data.name: Spore Cloud
enemies_data.description: Not a creature exactly — a living cloud of luminous spores that drifts with purpose. Inhaling them causes disorientation and hallucinations.
aspects: One Breath and the World Goes Sideways
It Goes Where the Air Goes

frozen_wreck.name: The Frozen Wreck
frozen_wreck.description: An ancient ship, petrified and encrusted with void-ice. It drifts in eerie silence, its surfaces glittering with frost. Whatever happened here happened long ago, and some things are best left undisturbed.
frozen_wreck.aspect: The Cold Preserves What Should Have Been Forgotten

rooms.name: Ice-Crusted Bow
rooms.description: The prow of the ancient vessel juts from a mass of void-ice. The metal beneath is unlike anything you've seen — dark, dense, and covered in script that seems to shift when you're not looking directly at it. The way back is to the south.
aspects: The Writing Changes When You Look Away
Built By Hands That Knew More Than Ours

rooms.name: Frost-Locked Corridor
rooms.description: A long hallway sheathed in ice so thick the walls are barely visible. Your breath freezes in the air. Shadows move beneath the ice — whether preserved crew or something else, you can't tell.
aspects: Cold Enough to Steal Your Thoughts
Something Watches From Beneath the Ice

rooms.name: Observation Deck
rooms.description: A domed room with panels of translucent crystal, now frosted over. Through the ice you can see the void stretching in all directions. A figure sits at a desk covered in notes and rubbings of the ship's inscriptions.
aspects: The Void Stares Back Through Frosted Glass
Every Surface Covered in Someone's Obsession

rooms.name: Crew Quarters
rooms.description: Rows of alcoves line the walls, each containing a frozen shape that might once have been a sleeping berth — or a coffin. The ice here is thinnest, and you can almost make out faces.
aspects: One Crack and Everything Comes Through

rooms.name: Ancient Armory
rooms.description: Weapon racks line the walls, but the weapons have long since fused with the ice. Only one corner remains accessible, where a warrior has set up camp amid the frozen arsenal.
aspects: Someone Here Is Ready for a Fight

rooms.name: The Vault
rooms.description: The deepest chamber of the ancient ship. The ice here is clear as glass, and through it you can see the walls are covered in intricate murals depicting a civilization of tree-like beings. At the center, encased in a pillar of crystal, rests a small structure — perfect, ornate, unmistakably a house built for beings no larger than your hand.
aspects: The Walls Tell a Dead People's Story

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade
enemies_data.description: A shadow that moves beneath the ice, reaching through with freezing tendrils. It was something once — crew, passenger, prisoner. Now it's just cold and hunger.
aspects: Reaches Through What Should Be Solid

enemies_data.name: Swarm Mites
enemies_data.description: Tiny crystalline insects that swarm in vast numbers. Each one is fragile, but together they can strip metal to the bone.
aspects: Tiny Jaws That Strip Metal Bare

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade Elder
enemies_data.description: The captain of the frozen wreck, preserved in void-ice for centuries. Its form is massive, translucent, and filled with a terrible cold intelligence. It guards the vault and the Eliok House within.
aspects: Still Thinks This Ship Is Its Command
Centuries of Cold Made It Patient
Nothing Leaves the Vault Without Permission

verdant_wreck.name: The Verdant Wreck
verdant_wreck.description: A massive biodome ship fragment — the growing section of a colony ship. When it broke apart, the growth systems kept running, fueled by void energy. Now it's a small jungle floating in nothing.

rooms.name: Overgrown Airlock
rooms.description: Roots crack through hull plates, splitting seams that were never meant to open. Warm, humid air rushes past you — the smell of soil and green things, impossibly alive in the void. The airlock frame is half-buried in moss.

rooms.name: The Promenade
rooms.description: A wide corridor lined with vine-covered benches and faded directional signs. One points north to GREENHOUSE, another west to AQUAPONICS. The floor tiles have buckled under root pressure, and bioluminescent fungus traces the cracks like veins.
aspects: Echoes of a Crew Long Gone

rooms.name: Aquaponics Tanks
rooms.description: Rows of cracked tanks line the walls, murky water still circulating through some ancient pump system. Bioluminescent eels dart between the tanks, and mushroom clusters sprout where water drips onto the deck.

rooms.name: The Nursery
rooms.description: Shattered seed trays cover every surface. Seedlings that were never meant to grow this large have pressed their canopies against the ceiling, cracking panels overhead. Thick pollen drifts in the air like snow. Someone left notes scattered across a workbench.

rooms.name: The Greenhouse
rooms.description: The main growing dome — transparent panels overhead reveal raw void, stars scattered behind condensation. Below, a riot of green. Planter beds overflow with vegetation, and cleared paths between them suggest someone has been tending this place.

rooms.name: The Canopy
rooms.description: Trees have grown so tall here they form a dense canopy, vines hanging like curtains between the trunks. The light is dim and green-filtered. Something rustles above — thick tendrils shifting between the branches.

rooms.name: Root Wall
rooms.description: The corridor ends in a wall of interwoven roots, each as thick as a person's torso. They pulse with a slow green light, and something glows beyond them — deep and warm. The roots are too dense to push through.

features.name: Roots
features.description: Massive interwoven roots, each thicker than your arm, pulsing with slow green bioluminescence. The outer bark is tough and damp — resistant to cutting or burning. But the surface has a waxy sheen that might react to the right solvent.

north.locked_desc: Massive roots block the passage north. They're alive and pulsing with green energy — too thick and damp to cut, too dense to squeeze through.

rooms.name: Observation Platform
rooms.description: A metal platform jutting above the tree line. Through cracked dome panels, void stretches in every direction — the skerry a distant speck of light. From up here, you can see the entire root system threading through the biodome like a circulatory system, all converging on something to the north.

rooms.name: Growth Control Room
rooms.description: A cramped room behind a maintenance hatch. Dead consoles line the walls, but one panel still flickers — the Growth Controller. Its screen shows a schematic of the biodome's root network, most of it pulsing red.

features.name: Growth Controller Console
features.description: A flickering console showing a schematic of the biodome's root network. Most nodes pulse red — overgrown, unresponsive. The panel below is dented but not sealed. With the right tools, you could get inside and repair the circuits.

rooms.name: The Verdant Heart
rooms.description: The biodome's core. An enormous plant fills the chamber, crystallized mid-bloom — petals frozen in a spiral of green and gold light. Its roots thread through every wall, every floor, the entire ship. At its base, a single bud glows with concentrated life energy.

features.name: Crystallized Bloom
features.description: An enormous flower frozen mid-bloom, petals spiraling outward in crystallized green and gold. It hums faintly. At its base, a single living bud pulses with concentrated energy — small enough to carry, if you could detach it.

enemies_data.name: Tangle-Vine
enemies_data.description: Thick tendrils lash out from the canopy above — a plant that's learned to hunt.
